title: Organization Creation
summary: Build the parent and child org tree first — users and data attach to organizations, so this is foundational.
customerSummary: We set up your organization — and any additional offices — as the foundation your team and data live in.
overview:
Organizations are the foundation. **Users and all data attach to orgs**, so the org tree must exist and be verified before you create users or load anything.

The model: a **parent organization** (the client company) with optional **child organizations** for offices and line cards. Reps live inside the correct node of that hierarchy.
prerequisites: Org structure documented: offices, line cards, hierarchy, rep roster
Account Type confirmed (Pilot vs Full License)
tips: Match the Company name to the Reference Sheet **exactly** — it appears throughout the platform and on client-facing artifacts.
Set Account Type before inviting users; it can drive different feature/seat settings.
warnings: This is a verification gate. Do not create users or load data until CS has signed off on the full org tree.
commonMistakes: Creating orphaned child orgs not nested under the parent.
Misspelling the company name so it no longer matches the contract.
Skipping the CS review gate and discovering hierarchy errors after users are added.

steps.title: Log into BuildVision
steps.description: Log in to your BuildVision account to get started.
steps.verify: You can sign in and reach your dashboard.

steps.title: Apply organization settings & account type
steps.description: Configure organization settings and apply the account type — Pilot vs Full License.
steps.verify: Feature/seat configuration matches the contract tier.

callouts.text: Pilot vs Full License may drive different feature/seat settings — set this before inviting users.

steps.title: Create child organizations
steps.description: Create offices / line cards as child orgs nested under the parent, if applicable.
steps.verify: Hierarchy renders as intended; no orphaned orgs.

screenshots.caption: Settings → Company shows every office and subsidiary under the root account — with type, users, phone, and address.
